compiled_state.description:
## What
The 0ac6 slice-3 review found a 10th JIRA_* consumer NOT routed through the typed Config: `src/rebar/_engine_support/bridge_fsck.py` (enumerate_open_count_skew_anomalies, ~lines 279-291) still reads JIRA_URL/JIRA_USER/JIRA_PROJECT/JIRA_API_TOKEN directly from os.environ when constructing an AcliClient. It is OUTSIDE the rebar_reconciler package (_engine_support) and its own comment notes it is NOT on any live path, so it was deliberately left out of slice 3's enumerated 9 reconciler sites.

Consequence: a [tool.rebar.jira] config-file value is consumed everywhere in the reconciler but NOT by this bridge_fsck anomaly helper — a minor consistency gap.

## Scope / consideration
- Route the url/user/project through the typed Config (env JIRA_URL/USER/PROJECT override file); keep the secret JIRA_API_TOKEN env-only.
- NOTE the cross-package wrinkle: bridge_fsck is in _engine_support and resolve_jira_settings lives in the reconciler floor (rebar_reconciler.acli_subprocess). Either (a) import the reconciler floor, or (b) resolve inline via `from rebar.config import load_config` (no reconciler import) — prefer whichever keeps _engine_support decoupled from the reconciler package.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] bridge_fsck's AcliClient url/user/project come from load_config().jira.* (env overrides file); JIRA_API_TOKEN stays env-only.
- [ ] A test asserts a [tool.rebar.jira] file value reaches that consumer (or, if it stays env-only by design, document why).
- [ ] No new _engine_support -> rebar_reconciler import coupling unless justified.
compiled_state.title: Route bridge_fsck's AcliClient construction through resolve_jira_settings (jira config-file)
